name: Restore Tab Key

releases.author: jrymk
releases.description: Fixed lists environment only works on list-1 (level 1 lists) and not indented lists (level 2 lists, list-2, and so on)
releases.name: release 1.18.6

releases.author: jrymk
releases.description:
### Streamlined plugin activation in different Markdown environments.
suggested in #14 
![image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/67e14b44-e6f6-4884-8985-4711f7323335)

You can still keep using the exception regex to indent when the list is empty by turning Lists **on** and enable the exception regex with the default regex. Turning Lists **off** will make it indent regardless. To access the regex settings, you will need to enable developer mode now. I'm not sure how the deprecated settings will behave on existing users. If things don't work right, try reinstalling the plugin.
releases.name: release 1.18.5

releases.author: jrymk
releases.description:
### Fix false triggers for brace matching, it now requires both opening and closing braces to be matched.

Yes, the options section is a disaster.

releases.name: release 1.15.1

releases.author: jrymk
releases.description:
# Only activate in code blocks is HERE!
All the compatibility stuff can be thrown out the window, if you only want to insert tabs in code blocks (as tabbing in any other places don't really do much, at least in the Markdown render), which I know people are using this plugin for. Now you can get IDE-like code editing experience in code blocks with proper tab/space insertion/indentation behavior, and just quick indenting for other stuff.

![image](https://github.com/jrymk/restore-tab-key/assets/39593345/d845316e-aa0d-4939-9e37-127dbd73b596)

The code block detection code is taken straight from the [Obsidian Tabout](https://github.com/phibr0/obsidian-tabout/blob/9f337685d070e68f70d51400ad9c4215c29e8f9a/src/main.ts#L60C14-L60C14) plugin. There is no way I could have figured this out on my own. Thank you so much phibr0.
releases.name: release 1.15.0

releases.author: jrymk
releases.description:
# Added Developer Mode

Added option for console output for debugging
![image](https://github.com/jrymk/restore-tab-key/assets/39593345/52aafff3-c7f0-4c92-898a-ded4bdd7fea0)

# Compatibility with Obsidian Table Editor introduced in v1.5.3
Under "Live Preview" mode, with the cursor on a table environment, enabling this compatibility (default) will not capture the tab key. The Advanced Table plugin also introduces bugs in the native table editor, so I personally recommending disabling Advanced Table or disable Tab binding for it, and edit tables in Live Preview mode.
This setting has no effect in Source mode.
![image](https://github.com/jrymk/restore-tab-key/assets/39593345/7ca2d321-b8c2-4cb5-8e9b-c35cc33e03bb)


# Deprecated Advanced Tables Plugin Compatibility

Advanced Tables now by defaults bind Tab in table environment with higher precedence than this plugin. There is no point calling `next-cell` from this plugin by disabling tab binding on Advanced Table plugin and then enable compatibility. The option can be show by enabling developer mode.
releases.name: release 1.14.1

releases.author: jrymk
releases.description:
# Added Developer Mode

Added option for console output for debugging
![image](https://github.com/jrymk/restore-tab-key/assets/39593345/52aafff3-c7f0-4c92-898a-ded4bdd7fea0)


# Deprecated Advanced Tables Plugin Compatibility

Advanced Table now by defaults bind Tab in table environment with higher precedence than this plugin. There is no point calling `next-cell` from this plugin by disabling tab binding on Advanced Table plugin and then enable compatibility. The option can be show by enabling developer mode.
releases.name: release 1.14.0

releases.author: jrymk
releases.description:
# Custom Tab Hotkey
You may now pick your own hotkey to trigger this plugin without replacing the default Tab key behavior.

---
![image](https://github.com/jrymk/restore-tab-key/assets/39593345/0a1e2efe-78e2-41e7-87cf-4043b2cb1eb5)
---


# Chinese Localization
I saw someone forked this repo for a Chinese readme (Yes, you, ACodeHX 😃). Well, I'm Taiwanese, I guess I can do something to make this plugin more accessible...
此更新新增简体和繁体中文选项。部分翻譯和中国大陆在地化参考了 ACodeHX。

![image](https://github.com/jrymk/restore-tab-key/assets/39593345/137206eb-359a-4fa6-a56e-a3657666c418)

---

# Updated default settings
I realized I left many settings' default really unreasonable, like I even commented this:
```js
	useHardSpace: false, // U+00A0 is technically not a space, let's not use it by default
```
and I still left it on. Why? Well during the development I just turned all the features on for testing, and guess who forgot to turn them back off.

- hard spaces are now off
- align spaces are now on

I also realize that many people use this plugin for code blocks, so the "use spaces instead of tab" can probably be on by default, but erm... whatever, it's a tab key, I promised it'll restore its behavior, let's insert a tab key by default.
releases.name: release 1.13.0

releases.author: jrymk
releases.description: Quick fix (#9) to Obsidian Outliner support causing recursive call.
releases.name: release 1.12.0

releases.author: jrymk
releases.description:
Implemented feature requests from #7 and #8
- Added option to only indent when selection contains multiple lines, and replaces selection with a tab within a single line, a behavior found in vscode and other editors
- Added option to align cursor with spaces, just like how tabs are rendered typically
releases.name: release 1.11.1

releases.author: jrymk
releases.description:
- Uses EditorExtension for key repeat and preventDefault functionality
You may now hold down Tab to insert multiple tabs
releases.name: release 1.10.0

releases.author: jrymk
releases.description: - Fix bug that breaks tab insertion due to Outliner indenting non-list text. Now Outliner compatibility only works on list items
releases.name: release 1.9.1

releases.author: jrymk
releases.description:
- Added Obsidian Outliner plugin compatibility
By default it is turned on. When tab key is pressed, `obsidian-outliner:indent-list` is called. If the line the cursor is on has changed, it probably means Outliner did its thing, and Restore Tab Key will not continue.
releases.name: release 1.9.0

releases.author: jrymk
releases.description:
- Simplified regex for tab exception
- Include checkboxes as tab exception (optional `[ ]` after `- ` or `1. `)
- Allow any number of whitespaces (spaces, tabs, or hard spaces) before and after `-` (unordered lists) and `1.` (ordered lists)
releases.name: release 1.8.0

releases.author: jrymk
releases.description:
Added Advanced Tables plugin support.
When the cursor is in a table, `table-editor-obsidian:next-cell` is called instead.
releases.name: release 1.7.0

releases.author: jrymk
releases.description:
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/39593345/220233685-7ec9a1a7-e52c-4c60-ba85-00f26755bfea.png)
Added settings for toggling indent exception and customizing exception regex.
releases.name: release 1.6.1

releases.author: jrymk
releases.description:
- Now uses editorCallback instead of matching hotkeys ourselves. Uses `indentMore` for the default indenting behavior
- Lists (some indentation followed by `- ` or `number. `) will indent regardless of selection (must use `-` for lists, because why not) (must include a space in the end)
releases.name: release 1.6.0

releases.author: jrymk
releases.description:
- Indentation is now handled by Obsidian natively. Ability to use hard spaces for indentation is lost, but now indentation will work as well as before.

TODO: Indent anyways for Markdown lists
releases.name: release 1.4.0

releases.author: jrymk
releases.description:
Empty lines will not be indented when multiple lines selected. Lines with indentation is NOT an empty line.
This better reflects the behaviour of most IDEs.

Example:
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/39593345/212386584-c5a74a3a-ee6f-4632-a262-eedd67ea7a08.png)
after indenting:
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/39593345/212386691-c1ff97dc-6ea8-4a2e-bb92-44d72c556603.png)
releases.name: release 1.3.1

releases.author: jrymk
releases.description:
Tabs/indentation now respects Obsidian app options
[suggested by huyz](https://github.com/jrymk/obsidian-tab-key/issues/1)

Optimized code for better flexibility

- It may require two Shift+Tab presses to unindent if indentation size is set to over 4 spaces. Unindentation action is handled natively by Obsidian, so even they didn't account for this. (not my problem 🙈)
- If indenting multiple lines, it will require a Ctrl+Z undo action for each line indented. Don't see an option to modify multiple lines at once or set indentation through the Obsidian API (let's just say that the Obsidian API is pretty poorly documented)
releases.name: release 1.3.0

releases.author: jrymk
releases.description:
Because yes, Markdown ignores tabs and repeated spaces, I added a quick command to add 4 hard spaces (U+00A0), nothing special.

Live preview/source view: Tab skips to the next stop, exactly how tab should work
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/39593345/212302794-2e59a369-447d-4f35-b4a5-b9d9a2bf29c3.png)
Read mode: Tabs are ignored and treated like a space.
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/39593345/212302824-3cf46a96-2ab5-4f62-b0f5-1af7e17ece09.png)

I might add an option to make the tab key insert 4 hard spaces instead, but realistically though, it looks kind of wrong. It's not a tab, but simply a wide horizontal space, which is not that commonly used.
Also, now that live preview mode works so well, I believe most people that view notes in Obsidian will view notes in this mode, which means the tab character will work like intended.
For printing and read mode though, there is just no way for tab characters to behave correctly, because it is the markdown renderer that ignores tab and replace with one space. This is just a simple plugin that does one thing, I'm not going to touch MarkdownPostProcessor.
releases.name: release 1.2.1

releases.author: jrymk
releases.description:
Now uses Obsidian commands with hotkeys to trigger tab key event.
Will prevent pressing tab in IME from triggering.
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/39593345/212299546-bc695a4a-dba9-467b-ab47-f225949a3986.png)
releases.name: release 1.2.0
